steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage ends with a blank that must logically follow from the finding: effort expectancy (perceived ease of use) positively influences users' intention to adopt premium streaming services. The correct completion must be a reasonable implication of that specific finding.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — it may be advantageous for music streaming companies to try to influence the extent to which potential users perceive the use of the premium version as effortless.: If perceived ease drives adoption, then shaping that perception is a sound business strategy. This follows directly from the finding. ✓
  • Choice B — participants who strongly agreed with the statement "I find paid music streaming services easy to use" were more likely to express loyalty to Spotify than to other streaming services.: The study measured intention to adopt premium services, not loyalty to a specific platform like Spotify. This goes beyond the study's scope. ✗
  • Choice C — users' intentions to pay for premium streaming services are probably unaffected by factors other than effort expectancy.: Finding that effort expectancy matters does not mean no other factors matter. This overstates the conclusion. ✗
  • Choice D — the statement "I find paid music streaming services easy to use" is not a reliable measure of effort expectancy.: The researchers used that statement as their measure of effort expectancy and found the expected result, so this contradicts the study's logic. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
